contentBlocks.original:
न मन्त्रं नो यन्त्रं तदपि च न जाने स्तुतिमहो
न चाह्वानं ध्यानं तदपि च न जाने स्तुतिकथाः ।
न जाने मुद्रास्ते तदपि च न जाने विलपनं
परं जाने मातस्त्वदनुसरणं क्लेशहरणम् ॥१॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na mantraṃ no yantraṃ tadapi ca na jāne stutimaho
na cāhvānaṃ dhyānaṃ tadapi ca na jāne stutikathāḥ .
na jāne mudrāste tadapi ca na jāne vilapanaṃ
paraṃ jāne mātastvadanusaraṇaṃ kleśaharaṇam ..1..
contentBlocks.translation:
(O Mother) neither your mantra, nor yantra (do I know); and alas, not even I know your stutI (eulogy),
I do not know how to invoke you through dhyana (meditation); (and alas), not even I know how to simply recite your glories (stuti-katha),
I do not know your mudras (to contemplate on you); (and alas), not even I know how to simply cry for you,
However, one thing I know (for certain); by following you (somehow through rememberance however imperfectly) will take away all my afflictions (from my mind).
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; मन्त्रं (mantraṃ) – mantra; नो (no) – nor; यन्त्रं (yantraṃ) – yantra; तदपि (tadapi) – even that (tad:that | api:even); च (ca) – and; न (na) – not; जाने (jāne) – I know; स्तुतिमहो (stutimaho) – eulogy, alas (stuti:eulogy | maho:alas); न (na) – not; चाह्वानं (cāhvānaṃ) – invocation; ध्यानं (dhyānaṃ) – meditation; तदपि (tadapi) – even that (tad:that | api:even); च (ca) – and; न (na) – not; जाने (jāne) – I know; स्तुतिकथाः (stutikathāḥ) – glory recitations (stuti:glory | kathāḥ:recitations); न (na) – not; जाने (jāne) – I know; मुद्रास्ते (mudrāste) – your mudras (mudrāḥ:mudras | te:your); तदपि (tadapi) – even that (tad:that | api:even); च (ca) – and; न (na) – not; जाने (jāne) – I know; विलपनं (vilapanaṃ) – crying; परं (paraṃ) – however; जाने (jāne) – I know; मातः (mātaḥ) – O Mother; त्वदनुसरणं (tvadanusaraṇaṃ) – following you (tvad:you | anusaraṇaṃ:following); क्लेशहरणम् (kleśaharaṇam) – removal of afflictions (kleśa:afflictions | haraṇam:removal);

contentBlocks.original:
विधेरज्ञानेन द्रविणविरहेणालसतया
विधेयाशक्यत्वात्तव चरणयोर्या च्युतिरभूत् ।
तदेतत् क्षन्तव्यं जननि सकलोद्धारिणि शिवे
कुपुत्रो जायेत क्वचिदपि कुमाता न भवति ॥२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidherajñānena draviṇaviraheṇālasatayā
vidheyāśakyatvāttava caraṇayoryā cyutirabhūt .
tadetat kṣantavyaṃ janani sakaloddhāriṇi śive
kuputro jāyeta kvacidapi kumātā na bhavati ..2..
contentBlocks.translation:
(O Mother) due to ignorance of the vidhis (injunctions of worship), and due to lack of wealth, as well as due to my indolent (lazy) nature, ...
it was not possible for me to serve your lotus feet; there have been failures on the performance of my duties (I admit that),
(but) all these are pardonable (by you), O Mother; because you are the saviour of all, O Shivaa (auspicious mother),
there can be Kuputra (fallen disobedient son turning away from mother), but there can never be Kumata (mother turning away from son permanently).
contentBlocks.meanings: विधेः (vidheḥ) – of the injunctions; अज्ञानेन (ajñānena) – by ignorance; द्रविणविरहेण (draviṇaviraheṇa) – due to lack of wealth (draviṇa:wealth | viraheṇa:due to lack); आलसतया (ālasatayā) – due to laziness; विधेयाशक्यत्वात् (vidheyāśakyatvāt) – due to inability to perform duties (vidheya:duties | aśakyatvāt:due to inability); तव (tava) – your; चरणयोः (caraṇayoḥ) – of the feet; या (yā) – which; च्युतिः (cyutiḥ) – falling away; अभूत् (abhūt) – happened; तत् (tat) – that; एतत् (etat) – this; क्षन्तव्यम् (kṣantavyam) – to be forgiven; जननि (janani) – O Mother; सकलोद्धारिणि (sakaloddhāriṇi) – saviour of all (sakala:all | uddhāriṇi:saviour); शिवे (śive) – O auspicious one; कुपुत्रः (kuputraḥ) – bad son; जायेत (jāyeta) – may be born; क्वचित् (kvacit) – somewhere; अपि (api) – even; कुमाता (kumātā) – bad mother; न (na) – not; भवति (bhavati) – exists;

contentBlocks.original:
पृथिव्यां पुत्रास्ते जननि बहवः सन्ति सरलाः
परं तेषां मध्ये विरलतरलोऽहं तव सुतः ।
मदीयोऽयं त्यागः समुचितमिदं नो तव शिवे
कुपुत्रो जायेत क्वचिदपि कुमाता न भवति ॥३॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pṛthivyāṃ putrāste janani bahavaḥ santi saralāḥ
paraṃ teṣāṃ madhye viralataralo'haṃ tava sutaḥ .
madīyo'yaṃ tyāgaḥ samucitamidaṃ no tava śive
kuputro jāyeta kvacidapi kumātā na bhavati ..3..
contentBlocks.translation:
(O Mother) in this world, there are many many sons of yours who are simple-minded,
however, among them I am a rare son of yours who is restless,
because of this only, it is not proper for you to forsake me O Shivaa (auspicious mother),
(because) there can be Kuputra (fallen disobedient son turning away from mother), but there can never be Kumata (mother turning away from son permanently).
contentBlocks.meanings: पृथिव्यां (pṛthivyāṃ) – on the earth; पुत्राः (putrāḥ) – sons; ते (te) – your; जननि (janani) – O Mother; बहवः (bahavaḥ) – many; सन्ति (santi) – are; सरलाः (saralāḥ) – simple-minded; परं (paraṃ) – however; तेषां (teṣāṃ) – among them; मध्ये (madhye) – among; विरलतरलः (viralataralaḥ) – rare and restless (virala:rare | taralaḥ:restless); अहम् (aham) – I; तव (tava) – your; सुतः (sutaḥ) – son; मदीयः (madīyaḥ) – my; अयम् (ayam) – this; त्यागः (tyāgaḥ) – forsaking; समुचितम् (samucitam) – proper; इदं (idaṃ) – this; नः (naḥ) – not; तव (tava) – your; शिवे (śive) – O auspicious one; कुपुत्रः (kuputraḥ) – bad son (ku:bad | putraḥ:son); जायेत (jāyeta) – may be born; क्वचित् (kvacit) – somewhere; अपि (api) – even; कुमाता (kumātā) – bad mother (ku:bad | mātā:mother); न (na) – not; भवति (bhavati) – exists;

contentBlocks.original:
जगन्मातर्मातस्तव चरणसेवा न रचिता
न वा दत्तं देवि द्रविणमपि भूयस्तव मया ।
तथापि त्वं स्नेहं मयि निरुपमं यत्प्रकुरुषे
कुपुत्रो जायेत क्वचिदपि कुमाता न भवति ॥४॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jaganmātarmātastava caraṇasevā na racitā
na vā dattaṃ devi draviṇamapi bhūyastava mayā .
tathāpi tvaṃ snehaṃ mayi nirupamaṃ yatprakuruṣe
kuputro jāyeta kvacidapi kumātā na bhavati ..4..
contentBlocks.translation:
O Jaganmata (mother of the world), O Mother, I have never served your lotus feet,
neither have I offered, O Devi, abundant wealth at your lotus feet (during worship),
inspite of this, you have maintained your motherly love towards me which is incomparable,
(because) there can be Kuputra (fallen disobedient son turning away from mother), but there can never be Kumata (mother turning away from son permanently).
contentBlocks.meanings: जगन्मातर् (jaganmātar) – O mother of the world (jagat:world | mātṛ:mother); मातः (mātaḥ) – O Mother; तव (tava) – your; चरणसेवा (caraṇasevā) – service to feet (caraṇa:feet | sevā:service); न (na) – not; रचिता (racitā) – performed; न (na) – not; वा (vā) – or; दत्तं (dattaṃ) – given; देवि (devi) – O Devi; द्रविणमपि (draviṇamapi) – even wealth (draviṇa:wealth | api:even); भूयः (bhūyaḥ) – abundant; तव (tava) – your; मया (mayā) – by me; तथापि (tathāpi) – inspite of this; त्वं (tvaṃ) – you; स्नेहं (snehaṃ) – love; मयि (mayi) – towards me; निरुपमं (nirupamaṃ) – incomparable; यत् (yat) – which; प्रकुरुषे (prakuruṣe) – you maintain; कुपुत्रः (kuputraḥ) – bad son (ku:bad | putra:son); जायेत (jāyeta) – may be born; क्वचित् (kvacit) – somewhere; अपि (api) – even; कुमाता (kumātā) – bad mother (ku:bad | mātā:mother); न (na) – not; भवति (bhavati) – exists;

contentBlocks.original:
परित्यक्ता देवा विविधविधसेवाकुलतया
मया पञ्चाशीतेरधिकमपनीते तु वयसि ।
इदानीं चेन्मातस्तव यदि कृपा नापि भविता
निरालम्बो लम्बोदरजननि कं यामि शरणम् ॥५॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
parityaktā devā vividhavidhasevākulatayā
mayā pañcāśīteradhikamapanīte tu vayasi .
idānīṃ cenmātastava yadi kṛpā nāpi bhavitā
nirālambo lambodarajanani kaṃ yāmi śaraṇam ..5..
contentBlocks.translation:
(O Mother) letting go (i.e. left or never undertaking) the various ritualistic worship services of the devas ...
by me, more than eighty five years of my life has passed,
even at this moment (nearing death), if your grace do not descend, O Mother (who is) of the form of bliss-consciousness, ...
where will this niralamba (One without any support) seek refuge, O Lambodara JananI (mother of Lambodara or Ganesha).
contentBlocks.meanings: परित्यक्ता (parityaktā) – abandoned; देवाः (devāḥ) – gods; विविधविधसेवाकुलतया (vividhavidhasevākulatayā) – due to various ritual services (vividha:various | vidha:types | sevā:services | kulatā:due to being occupied); मया (mayā) – by me; पञ्चाशीतेः (pañcāśīteḥ) – eighty-five; अधिकम् (adhikam) – more than; अपनीते (apanīte) – has passed; तु (tu) – but; वयसि (vayasi) – in age; इदानीम् (idānīm) – now; चेत् (cet) – if; मातः (mātaḥ) – O Mother; तव (tava) – your; यदि (yadi) – if; कृपा (kṛpā) – grace; न (na) – not; अपि (api) – even; भविता (bhavitā) – will be; निरालम्बः (nirālambaḥ) – without support; लम्बोदरजननि (lambodarajanani) – mother of Lambodara (lambodara:Lambodara (Ganesha) | janani:mother); कं (kaṃ) – whom; यामि (yāmi) – shall I go; शरणम् (śaraṇam) – refuge;

contentBlocks.original:
श्वपाको जल्पाको भवति मधुपाकोपमगिरा
निरातङ्को रङ्को विहरति चिरं कोटिकनकैः ।
तवापर्णे कर्णे विशति मनुवर्णे फलमिदं
जनः को जानीते जननि जपनीयं जपविधौ ॥६॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śvapāko jalpāko bhavati madhupākopamagirā
nirātaṅko raṅko viharati ciraṃ koṭikanakaiḥ .
tavāparṇe karṇe viśati manuvarṇe phalamidaṃ
janaḥ ko jānīte janani japanīyaṃ japavidhau ..6..
contentBlocks.translation:
(O Mother) a swapaka (a dog-eater or chandala) (from whose mouth nothing much comes out in terms of good speech) becomes jalpaka (talkative) with speech like a madhupaka (from whose mouth good speech comes out like honey) (by your grace),
a ranka (poor and miserable) becomes niratanka (free from fear) forever, and moves about having obtained million gold (by your grace),
O Aparna (another name of DevI Parvati), when your prayer (and glory) enter one's ear (and sits in the heart), such is the result,
(then) who among men can know, O Mother, the destiny which your holy japa can unfold?
contentBlocks.meanings: श्वपाकः (śvapākaḥ) – dog-eater; जल्पाकः (jalpākaḥ) – talkative; भवति (bhavati) – becomes; मधुपाक-उपम-गिरा (madhupāka-upama-girā) – with speech like honey (madhupāka:honey-like | upama:like | girā:with speech); निरातङ्कः (nirātaṅkaḥ) – free from fear; रङ्कः (raṅkaḥ) – poor person; विहरति (viharati) – moves about; चिरम् (ciraṃ) – for a long time; कोटि-कनकैः (koṭi-kanakaiḥ) – with million gold (koṭi:million | kanakaiḥ:with gold); तव (tava) – your; अपर्णे (aparṇe) – O Aparna; कर्णे (karṇe) – in the ear; विशति (viśati) – enters; मनु-वर्णे (manu-varṇe) – in the form of mantra (manu:mantra | varṇe:in the form); फलम् (phalam) – result; इदं (idaṃ) – this; जनः (janaḥ) – person; कः (kaḥ) – who; जानीते (jānīte) – knows; जननि (janani) – O Mother; जपनीयम् (japanīyam) – to be chanted; जपविधौ (japavidhau) – in the method of chanting;

contentBlocks.original:
चिताभस्मालेपो गरलमशनं दिक्पटधरो
जटाधारी कण्ठे भुजगपतिहारी पशुपतिः ।
कपाली भूतेशो भजति जगदीशैकपदवीं
भवानि त्वत्पाणिग्रहणपरिपाटीफलमिदम् ॥७॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
citābhasmālepo garalamaśanaṃ dikpaṭadharo
jaṭādhārī kaṇṭhe bhujagapatihārī paśupatiḥ .
kapālī bhūteśo bhajati jagadīśaikapadavīṃ
bhavāni tvatpāṇigrahaṇaparipāṭīphalamidam ..7..
contentBlocks.translation:
(O Mother) (Lord Shankara), who is smeared with chitabhasma (ashes from the cremation ground), whose food is the poison, whose clothes are the directions, ...
who has matted hairs on his head, who wears the garland of the king of snakes around his neck; (inspite of all this he is called) PashupatI (the lord of the pashus or living beings),
he carries a begging bowl of skull in his hand but is worshipped as Bhutesha (the lord of the bhutas or beings) and got the title of Jagadisha eka (One lord of the universe), ...
O Bhavani, all this is because of the result of your panI grahana (accepting your hand in marriage).
contentBlocks.meanings: चिताभस्मालेपः (citābhasmālepaḥ) – smeared with cremation ashes (citā:cremation | bhasma:ashes | ālepaḥ:smeared with); गरलमशनम् (garalamaśanam) – poison as food (garala:poison | aśanam:food); दिक्पटधरः (dikpaṭadharaḥ) – wearing directions as clothes (dik:directions | paṭa:cloth | dharaḥ:wearing); जटाधारी (jaṭādhārī) – having matted hair (jaṭā:matted hair | dhārī:having); कण्ठे (kaṇṭhe) – on the neck; भुजगपतिहारी (bhujagapatihārī) – wearing snake king garland (bhujaga:snake | pati:king | hārī:garland); पशुपतिः (paśupatiḥ) – lord of creatures (paśu:creatures | patiḥ:lord); कपाली (kapālī) – skull-bearer; भूतेशः (bhūteśaḥ) – lord of beings (bhūta:beings | īśaḥ:lord); भजति (bhajati) – worships; जगदीशैकपदवीं (jagadīśaikapadavīm) – title of one lord of universe (jagat:universe | īśa:lord | eka:one | padavīm:title); भवानि (bhavāni) – O Bhavani; त्वत्पाणिग्रहणपरिपाटीफलम् (tvatpāṇigrahaṇaparipāṭīphalam) – result of accepting your hand (tvat:your | pāṇi:hand | grahaṇa:accepting | paripāṭī:procedure | phalam:result); इदम् (idam) – this;

contentBlocks.original:
न मोक्षस्याकाङ्क्षा भवविभववाञ्छापि च न मे
न विज्ञानापेक्षा शशिमुखि सुखेच्छापि न पुनः ।
अतस्त्वां संयाचे जननि जननं यातु मम वै
मृडानी रुद्राणी शिव शिव भवानीति जपतः ॥८॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na mokṣasyākāṅkṣā bhavavibhavavāñchāpi ca na me
na vijñānāpekṣā śaśimukhi sukhecchāpi na punaḥ .
atastvāṃ saṃyāce janani jananaṃ yātu mama vai
mṛḍānī rudrāṇī śiva śiva bhavānīti japataḥ ..8..
contentBlocks.translation:
(O Mother) I do not have the desire for moksha (liberation); neither have I the desire for worldly fortune,
neither do I long for worldly knowledge, O ShashI MukhI (the moon-faced one); I do not have the desire for enjoying the worldly comforts again,
henceforth I implore you, O Mother, may you direct my life towards (the rememberance of your names),
(the string of your holy names) MridanI RudranI Shiva Shiva Bhavani; may my future life be spent in performing japa of your holy names.
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; मोक्षस्य (mokṣasya) – of liberation; आकाङ्क्षा (ākāṅkṣā) – desire; भवविभववाञ्छा (bhavavibhavavāñchā) – desire for worldly fortune (bhava:worldly | vibhava:fortune | vāñchā:desire); अपि (api) – also; च (ca) – and; न (na) – not; मे (me) – my; न (na) – not; विज्ञानापेक्षा (vijñānāpekṣā) – desire for knowledge (vijñāna:knowledge | āpekṣā:desire); शशिमुखि (śaśimukhi) – O moon-faced one (śaśi:moon | mukhi:faced); सुखेच्छा (sukhecchā) – desire for comfort (sukha:comfort | icchā:desire); अपि (api) – also; न (na) – not; पुनः (punaḥ) – again; अतः (ataḥ) – therefore; त्वां (tvāṃ) – you; संयाचे (saṃyāce) – implore; जननि (janani) – O Mother; जननं (jananaṃ) – birth; यातु (yātu) – may go; मम (mama) – my; वै (vai) – indeed; मृडानी (mṛḍānī) – Mṛḍānī; रुद्राणी (rudrāṇī) – Rudrāṇī; शिव (śiva) – Shiva; शिव (śiva) – Shiva; भवानीति (bhavānīti) – Bhavānī; जपतः (japataḥ) – chanting;

contentBlocks.original:
नाराधितासि विधिना विविधोपचारैः
किं रुक्षचिन्तनपरैर्न कृतं वचोभिः ।
श्यामे त्वमेव यदि किञ्चन मय्यनाथे
धत्से कृपामुचितमम्ब परं तवैव ॥९॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārādhitāsi vidhinā vividhopacāraiḥ
kiṃ rukṣacintanaparairna kṛtaṃ vacobhiḥ .
śyāme tvameva yadi kiñcana mayyanāthe
dhatse kṛpāmucitamamba paraṃ tavaiva ..9..
contentBlocks.translation:
(O Mother) I have not worshipped you as prescribed by tradition with various rituals,
(On the other hand) what rough thoughts did my mind not think and my speech utter?
O Shyama, inspite of this, if you indeed, to a little extent, to this orphan ...
have extended your grace, O supreme mother, it indeed only becomes you (i.e. is possible for you).
contentBlocks.meanings: न (na) – not; आराधिता (ārādhitā) – worshipped; असि (asi) – you are; विधिना (vidhinā) – by tradition; विविधोपचारैः (vividhopacāraiḥ) – with various rituals (vividha:various | upacāraiḥ:with rituals); किं (kiṃ) – what; रुक्षचिन्तनपरैः (rukṣacintanaparaiḥ) – with rough thoughts (rukṣa:rough | cintana:thoughts | paraiḥ:with); न (na) – not; कृतं (kṛtaṃ) – done; वचोभिः (vacobhiḥ) – by words; श्यामे (śyāme) – O Shyama; त्वम् (tvam) – you; एव (eva) – indeed; यदि (yadi) – if; किञ्चन (kiñcana) – to some extent; मयि (mayi) – in me; अनाथे (anāthe) – orphan; धत्से (dhatse) – extend; कृपाम् (kṛpām) – grace; उचितम् (ucitam) – appropriate; अम्ब (amba) – O Mother; परं (paraṃ) – supreme; तव (tava) – your; एव (eva) – indeed;

contentBlocks.original:
आपत्सु मग्नः स्मरणं त्वदीयं
करोमि दुर्गे करुणार्णवेशि ।
नैतच्छठत्वं मम भावयेथाः
क्षुधातृषार्ता जननीं स्मरन्ति ॥१०॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āpatsu magnaḥ smaraṇaṃ tvadīyaṃ
karomi durge karuṇārṇaveśi .
naitacchaṭhatvaṃ mama bhāvayethāḥ
kṣudhātṛṣārtā jananīṃ smaranti ..10..
contentBlocks.translation:
(O Mother) I have sunk in misfortunes and therefore remembering you now (which I never did before),
O Mother Durga, (you who are) an ocean of compassion, ...
(therefore) do not think of me as false (and my invocation as pretence),
(because) when children are afflicted with hunger and thirst, they naturally remember their mother (Only).
contentBlocks.meanings: आपत्सु (āpatsu) – in misfortunes; मग्नः (magnaḥ) – sunk; स्मरणं (smaraṇam) – remembrance; त्वदीयं (tvadīyam) – your; करोमि (karomi) – I do; दुर्गे (durge) – O Durga; करुणार्णवेशि (karuṇārṇaveśi) – O ocean of compassion (karuṇā:compassion | arṇava:ocean | īśi:O ruler); न (na) – not; एतच्छठत्वं (etacchaṭhatvam) – this deceit (etat:this | chaṭhatvam:deceit); मम (mama) – my; भावयेथाः (bhāvayethāḥ) – consider; क्षुधातृषार्ता (kṣudhātṛṣārtā) – afflicted by hunger and thirst (kṣudhā:hunger | tṛṣā:thirst | ārtā:afflicted); जननीं (jananīm) – mother; स्मरन्ति (smaranti) – remember;

contentBlocks.original:
जगदम्ब विचित्रमत्र किं
परिपूर्णा करुणास्ति चेन्मयि ।
अपराधपरम्परापरं
न हि माता समुपेक्षते सुतम् ॥११॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jagadamba vicitramatra kiṃ
paripūrṇā karuṇāsti cenmayi .
aparādhaparamparāparaṃ
na hi mātā samupekṣate sutam ..11..
contentBlocks.translation:
O Jagadamba (mother of the universe), what is surprising in this!
the graceful compassion of the (blissful) mother always remains fully filled,
(because) inspite of the son committing mistakes after mistakes,
the mother never abandons the son.
contentBlocks.meanings: जगदम्ब (jagadamba) – O mother of the universe (jagat:universe | ambā:mother); विचित्रम् (vicitram) – surprising; अत्र (atra) – here; किं (kiṃ) – what; परिपूर्णा (paripūrṇā) – fully filled; करुणा (karuṇā) – compassion; अस्ति (asti) – is; चेत् (cet) – if; मयि (mayi) – in me; अपराधपरम्परापरम् (aparādha-paramparā-param) – mistakes after mistakes (aparādha:mistake | paramparā:series | param:after); न (na) – not; हि (hi) – indeed; माता (mātā) – mother; समुपेक्षते (samupekṣate) – abandons; सुतम् (sutam) – son;

contentBlocks.original:
मत्समः पातकी नास्ति पापघ्नी त्वत्समा न हि ।
एवं ज्ञात्वा महादेवि यथायोग्यं तथा कुरु ॥१२॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
matsamaḥ pātakī nāsti pāpaghnī tvatsamā na hi .
evaṃ jñātvā mahādevi yathāyogyaṃ tathā kuru ..12..
contentBlocks.translation:
(O Mother) there is no one as fallen like me, and there is no one as uplifting (by removing sins) like you,
considering thus, O Mahadevi, please do whatever is proper (to save me).
contentBlocks.meanings: मत्समः (matsamaḥ) – like me (mat:me | samaḥ:like); पातकी (pātakī) – sinner; नास्ति (nāsti) – there is not; पापघ्नी (pāpaghnī) – remover of sins (pāpa:sins | ghnī:remover); त्वत्समा (tvatsamā) – like you (tvat:you | samā:like); न (na) – not; हि (hi) – indeed; एवं (evaṃ) – thus; ज्ञात्वा (jñātvā) – knowing; महादेवि (mahādevi) – O great goddess (mahā:great | devi:goddess); यथायोग्यं (yathāyogyaṃ) – as appropriate (yathā:as | yogyaṃ:appropriate); तथा (tathā) – so; कुरु (kuru) – do;
